notes: WITH-SKILL: 100 percent pass rate across all 3 evals, zero variance. Every with-skill run produced the exact prescribed shape (function name TestRoundTripFromTestdata, table-driven testCases, t.Parallel at both levels, AST equality for text vs byte equality for binary).

BASELINE: 67.8 percent pass rate with high variance (stddev 28). Eval-2 baseline scored 100 percent only because the existing TestRoundTripFromTestdata in the fixture seeded the convention; once a convention is established, baselines tend to follow it.

WHERE BASELINE FELL SHORT: every baseline picked a different function name (TestFixtureRoundTrip, TestGoldenRoundTrip), and one used a glob-based zero-touch table instead of an explicit testCases slice. Both choices are defensible in isolation, but they fragment the test surface across packages.

COPY VS MOVE: all 3 baselines moved the source file into testdata/ (deleting the original); all 3 with-skill runs copied. The skill should make this explicit (currently the wording is implicit in 'copied verbatim') because it is a real divergence in behavior.

TIME/TOKEN COST: with-skill is 18.8s slower and uses 4159 more tokens on average. The cost is reading SKILL.md and applying its template verbatim. For a one-off task this is a meaningful overhead; for a fleet of file libraries that the user wants tested consistently, it is worth it.

BINARY-FAILURE FRAMING: eval-1 (binary, decoder still stubbed) expects the round-trip test to fail. Both with-skill and baseline correctly framed this as an implementation gap rather than a skill bug, citing errUnimplemented. The skill made this explicit; the baseline got it right anyway from context.

GRADER LIMITATION: my graders look up the round-trip test by exact name (TestRoundTripFromTestdata). When baselines used different names, downstream checks (uses os.ReadFile, table-driven, t.Parallel) reported as failing even when the substance was present. This makes the with-vs-baseline gap look slightly larger than it really is. The naming consistency itself is real value, just not the only value.
